set.caption: Fig. 4.9 Helicity. In (a) the spin and velocity are parallel (helicity +1); in (b) they are antiparallel (helicity -1).

overrides.reason: Figure 4.9 is split across two OCR image blocks: body[208] is subfigure (a), body[209] carries the shared caption plus subfigure (b). Promote body[209] as the semantic figure and expose aliases for refs 4.9a/4.9b.
Figure 4.11 is split into subfigure (a) at body[221] and caption-bearing subfigure (b) at body[223]. Keep body[221] excluded, and make body[223] the semantic figure with aliases for lettered refs.

set.caption: Fig. 9.3 (a) A typical semileptonic decay ($\overline{K}^0 \to \pi^+ + \mu^- + \overline{\nu}_\mu$). (b) A typical nonleptonic weak decay ($\Lambda \to p^+ + \pi^-$).

overrides.reason: Figure 9.3 uses a separate unlabeled subfigure block for (a) and a caption-bearing block for (b). Normalize the caption on body[239] and expose aliases for refs 9.3a/9.3b.

set.caption: Fig. 11.5 'Normal' and 'inverted' neutrino mass spectrum. The units are $(\mathrm{eV} / c^2)^2$ .

overrides.reason: The shared caption for Figure 11.5 was OCR'd with a stray leading 'Inverted'. Normalize it so build_catalogs can recover fig-11.5 while the companion 'Normal' subfigure block stays excluded.

block.caption: Fig. 1.13 The charmed baryon. The most probable interpretation of this event is v_μ + p → Λ_c^+ + μ^- + π^+ + π^-. The charmed baryon decays (Λ_c^+ → Λ + π^+) too soon to leave a track, but the subsequent decay of the Λ is clearly visible. (Photo courtesy of Samios, N. P. Brookhaven National Laboratory.)

overrides.reason: Raw PDF page 64 contains Fig. 1.13, but MinerU unified/parsed dropped the visual block entirely; insert a deterministic crop so catalog refs resolve.
